concepts.title: Conormal Points of ellipse
concepts.conceptBody:
Co-normal points

Let $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right),\text{Q}\left( {{\text{x}}_{2}},{{\text{y}}_{2}} \right),\text{R}\left( {{\text{x}}_{3}},{{\text{y}}_{3}} \right)$ and $\text{S}\left( {{\text{x}}_{4}},{{\text{y}}_{4}} \right)$ be the conormal points. Let normals at $\text{P},\text{Q}$, $\text{R},\text{S}$ meet in $\text{T}\left( \text{h},\text{k} \right)$.

Then equation of normal at $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ is

$\frac{{{a}^{2}}x}{{{x}_{1}}}-\frac{{{b}^{2}}y}{{{y}_{1}}}={{a}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}$

The point $\text{T}\left( \text{h},\text{k} \right)$ lies on it

$\left( {{a}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}} \right){{x}_{1}}{{y}_{1}}+{{b}^{2}}k{{x}_{1}}-{{a}^{2}}h{{y}_{1}}=0$

Similarly, for points $\text{Q},\text{R}$ and $\text{S}$ in equation (i), ${{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}}$ are just replaced by $\left( {{\text{x}}_{2}},{{\text{y}}_{2}} \right),\left( {{\text{x}}_{3}},{{\text{y}}_{3}} \right)$, $\left( {{\text{x}}_{4}},{{\text{y}}_{4}} \right)$ respectively.

∴ $\text{P},\text{Q},\text{R},\text{S}$ all lie on the curve

$\left( {{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{b}}^{2}} \right)\text{xy}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}\text{kx}-{{\text{a}}^{2}}\text{hy}=0$, which is called Apollonius a rectangular hyperbola.

examples.title: Let the normals at the four points $\left(x_1, y_1\right),\left(x_2, y_2\right),\left(x_3, y_3\right)$ and $\left(x_4, y_4\right)$ on the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ be concurrent at some point (called as conormal point). Then $\left(x_1+x_2+x_3+x_4\right)\left(\frac{1}{x_1}+\frac{1}{x_2}+\frac{1}{x_3}+\frac{1}{x_4}\right)$ is equal to
which of the following is properties of co normal of ellipse
Sum of eccentric angles $(\alpha, \beta, \gamma, \delta)$ of co-normal points of ellipse equals to an odd multiple of $\pi$.
how many normals are required to form co normal points
sum of ordinate points of co normal
